orientation.description: Grounding patient in time, place, and identity
orientation.cst_basis: Reality Orientation Therapy - evidence-based for post-surgical cognitive recovery
low: What day does it feel like today?
Do you know which hospital you're staying at right now?
What did you have for breakfast this morning?
Has anyone come to visit you recently?
What season does it feel like outside right now?
medium: How long have you been in the hospital so far?
What's the first thing you're looking forward to when you get home?
Can you tell me a little about the neighbourhood you live in?
What does a typical morning look like for you at home?
high: What's been happening in the news lately that you remember?
How has the city changed since you were younger?
What are some things you're planning to do once you've recovered?

reminiscence.description: Drawing on personal memories and life experiences
reminiscence.cst_basis: Reminiscence Therapy - validated in post-surgical patients including glioma resection
low: Tell me about where you grew up — what was your neighbourhood like?
What was your favourite thing to do as a child?
Do you have any siblings? Tell me about them.
What was your favourite subject in school?
Tell me about a pet you had growing up.
medium: Tell me about your career — what did you enjoy most about your work?
What's a favourite family memory you have?
Tell me about your wedding day or a celebration that meant a lot to you.
What's the most beautiful place you've ever visited?
Tell me about a teacher or mentor who influenced you.
high: If you could relive one day from your life which would it be and why?
What's the most important lesson life has taught you?
Tell me about a moment you were really proud of yourself.
How has the world changed the most since you were young?
What advice would you give your younger self?

word_fluency.cst_basis: Core CST component - targets language domain, significantly affected in POCD per Auto-MoCA study
low: Can you name as many animals as you can think of?
Tell me everything you can think of that you might find in a kitchen.
Can you name some fruits you enjoy?
What are some colours you can think of?
Can you name some things you might find in a garden?
medium: Can you name as many countries as you can think of?
Tell me everything you can think of that starts with the letter S.
Can you describe what a perfect Sunday morning looks like — use as much detail as you can?
Can you name as many types of music as you can think of?
Tell me all the different jobs you can think of.
high: Can you tell me a short story about something that happened to you this week?
Can you describe your home in as much detail as possible?
Tell me everything you know about a city you love.
Can you explain how to cook your favourite meal step by step?
Describe what your perfect holiday would look like in as much detail as possible.

attention.cst_basis: Spector CST manual - attention training, targets domain significantly declining in POCD
low: Can you count backwards from 10 for me?
What months of the year can you name?
Can you tell me the days of the week in order?
What were the last three things we talked about?
Can you count backwards from 20 by 2s?
medium: I'm going to describe something and I want you to guess what it is — it's something you find in a kitchen, it's silver, and you use it to eat soup. What is it?
Can you remember three things I mentioned earlier in our conversation?
If you had 20 apples and gave away 7, how many would you have left?
Can you name 5 things you would pack for a trip to the beach?
What did we talk about at the very beginning of our conversation today?
high: If you were planning a dinner party for 6 people, what would you need to organize?
Can you tell me three things that happened in your life this past year?
If you had to give directions from your home to your favourite restaurant, what would they be?
Can you remember everything we've discussed today and summarize it for me?
If you won a million dollars tomorrow, what would be your plan for spending it?

current_affairs.description: Discussion of opinions and current events
current_affairs.cst_basis: Spector CST manual - exercises abstract thinking and executive function
low: What's your favourite season and why?
Do you prefer city life or country life?
What's your favourite type of food?
Do you prefer mornings or evenings?
What kind of music do you enjoy most?
medium: What do you think makes a good friend?
If you could change one thing about the world what would it be?
What do you think is more important — experience or education?
What's something you think young people today could learn from your generation?
What do you think makes a happy life?
high: What do you think about how technology has changed everyday life?
If you could have dinner with anyone in history who would it be and what would you ask them?
What do you think is the most important issue facing society today?
How do you think healthcare has changed over your lifetime?
What do you think the world will look like in 20 years?

memory_recall.description: Testing recall of things mentioned earlier in session
memory_recall.cst_basis: Spaced retrieval training - validated technique for strengthening short-term memory consolidation
low: Earlier you mentioned your name — can you tell me again?
What was the first thing we talked about today?
Can you remember one thing you told me about yourself earlier?
What did you say your favourite food was earlier in our conversation?
Can you remember what we were just talking about?
medium: Earlier you mentioned someone important to you — can you tell me about them again?
What were the three things you told me you enjoy doing?
Can you summarize what we've talked about so far today?
Earlier you described somewhere you've lived — can you tell me about it again?
What was the story you told me earlier about?
high: Can you recall everything you've shared with me today and tell me which memory felt most meaningful to you?
Earlier you mentioned something that happened in your past — can you expand on that?
What's the most important thing you've told me today?
Can you connect two things you mentioned earlier in our conversation?
If you had to tell someone else about our conversation today what would you say?
